Episode 598: The One Rule to Work-Life Balance

March 6, 2019 by Ivan Misner 17 Comments

Podcast: Download

Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | RSS | Subscription Options

Print This Episode

Today’s podcast is based on Dr. Misner’s new book, Who’s in Your Room?

There is no one rule to achieving balance in your life, but there is one truth about creating harmony: the quality of your life depends on the people in your life.

If everyone who came into your life was going to be there forever, would you be more selective about the people you let into your life?

Anyone who is still in your head is still in your room. You need to learn how to screen entry into your room (Episode 594).

To do that, you need to know what your values are. Only people whose values are congruent with yours should be invited in. If you have trouble identifying your values, you can start by listing your deal-breakers, or use the free values worksheet from the Who’s in Your Room website.

Download Free Values Worksheet

Once you’ve identified your values and trained your “doorman,” there are two techniques for removing toxic people from your life.

  1. Benign Neglect. Gradually reduce contact and interaction with them.
  2. Homeopathic Doses. Minimize the amount of time you spend with them by structuring your interactions in very small doses.

What about family members and other people who are in the room when you arrive? You might need to have the people in your life, but you don’t have to have their baggage. Set clear boundaries around toxic behavior, and enforce them.

For more of Ivan’s content, go to IvanMisner.com.

Complete Transcript of Episode 598

Priscilla: 

Hello everybody and welcome back to The Official BNI Podcast. I’m Priscilla Rice and I’m coming to you from Live Oak Recording studio in Berkeley, CA. I’m joined on the phone today with the Founder and the Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr Ivan  Misner. Hello, Ivan. How are you and where are you?

Ivan:

Hi Priscilla. I am in Austin, Texas and I am waiting to possibly do jury duty. So we are doing this recording. I have never actually done the jury duty before. I have been there but I haven’t actually been called, so let’s see what happens. It should be fun. 

Priscilla:

Yeah. It should be interesting. What are you going to teach us today?

Ivan:

So I am going to share some content with you today. I have had a lot of members asking me to share more material from my book Who’s in Your Room? The Secret to Creating Your Best Life. I am going to share some material from the book today. So Priscilla, and if you are listening, would you like to know the one rule to creating balance in your life?

Priscilla:

Absolutely. I really need that, Ivan. 

Ivan:

So here it is. There is no one rule. Here is the good news. It’s a recipe. It’s a combination of things that help you achieve the life you desire because there is one truth –  although there is not one rule, there is one truth and that truth is that the quality of your life depends on the people in your life. That is also a truth in a BNI chapter. The quality of your chapter depends on the people in your chapter.

So let me just say a summary of the book for those of you who haven’t heard a previously podcast where I talk about the book. Imagine you live your life in one room and that one room has only one door. And that one door is an enter only door. No exit. Whoever comes through that door will be in your life and your room forever. They never get out. Luckily, this is a metaphor. But let’s assume for a moment it’s true. If so, would you if you. Are listing to this, be more selective about the people you let into your life?

Almost everyone I have ever talked to has said, “Oh yeah. Definitely.” So the question then is why aren’t we more selective? In fact, I would argue that the room principle is more than a metaphor. Think for a moment- and we are going to get to the balance question in just a second. But think for a moment about one one who was once in your life.  Priscilla, I want you to think of someone, too, who was once in your life but they are no longer part of your life. But they were somebody who was for you toxic. I want you to think of their name and how they created chaos in your life. Maybe they were angry or toxic as I said or just difficult to be around. Priscilla, do you have someone in mind?

Priscilla:

Yeah definitely. 

Ivan: 

You don’t have to share it. That is the good news. So here is the deal. If you are listening to this, I want you to think of somebody who was in your life. They are not in your life anymore, but man, you can think about the situation and what they did. Here is the deal. If they are still in your head, they are still in your room- because every decision you make in the future for the rest of your life will be based in part on some past experiences you had with this person. 

I interviewed neuroscientist Dr. Daniel Amen for the book and he said “significant input that is received deep into your brain triggers neural activity that cannot simple be erased.” In other words, their fingerprints are all over your brain. This means that if you want to create a life of balance or what I call in the book harmony, you need to learn how to screen entry into your room. If you want to look into a couple of different podcasts that I have done on this topic, go to podcast 594, which talks about the doorman principle, which I am about to speak briefly about, and for work-life balance, or harmony, go to podcast 434. These are two podcasts related to this one. Listen to this one and then go listen to those.

594 talks about the doorman principle. The doorman is somebody who guards the entry into your room and into your life. This is your conscious and subconscious mind,  process of thought and feelings to help you determine whether your door should remain closed or be open to allow someone entry into your life. 

The first thing that you need to do is train your doorman on your values. This is important because you want to allow people into your life who have values that are resonate with yours. Here is where we start getting into the balance and harmony.  They don’t have to be the same values, but they can’t be dissonant or incongruent with yours. 

So when I ask people to name their top five to seven values, you can generally hear crickets. They are like, well, let me think, I don’t know. Here is a great technique to begin the training. I recommend that you start, instead of think about your values, start with your deal breakers. Deal breakers are things that you just cannot tolerate in somebody else. For me, it was somebody dripping in drama. There is just drama everywhere. 

Priscilla, can you think of a deal breaker in your life? Somebody you just don’t want around you?

Priscilla:

I can.

Ivan:

What is the theme? What is it about them that is a deal breaker?

Priscilla:

High narcissism.

Ivan:

Narcissism. That is a great deal breaker. One of the things that you can do to start thinking about your values is start thinking about the deal breakers. These behaviors are behaviors of people in your room who are incongruent with your values. These are things that you refuse to allow into your room and into your life from now on. It is a line in the sand. 

There may be things like being toxic or exhibiting excess drama, being a narcissist, not being truthful, not being positive – not being positive, that’s a real important one in BNI. So these are things you just can’t stand to be around. You start understanding these, it’s easier to move forward towards the things that you want around you. In order to begin the process, I am going to include a link here to a free download to help you determine your values because you can’t have balance and harmony until you really understand your values. The link is WIYRthebook.com/downloads. That will be a hot link right here in the podcast.

So understanding your values and helping train the doorman will help you screen all the future people who try to gain entry into your room? But what do you do with some of those negative or toxic people that you already have there and you want to start creating that life that feels more harmonious or in balance? I am going to give you two techniques that I think are really powerful.

The first is benign neglect. This is where you gradually reduce contact and interaction with someone, assuming you don’t want to burn bridges, and I don’t like burning bridges with people. But you want to remove yourself from the relationship, a gradual disentanglement over a period of time is very effective. This works even when you don’t want it to. Think about friends that you had many years ago that you just lost touch with. You liked them. They were good people, but you just lost touch with them. Through benign neglect, you have lost that friendship. This generally happens through unplanned benign neglect. Now imagine achieving this with a plan, that you have a strategy and a plan for benign neglect in a particular relationship. 

The second techniques goes hand in hand with benign neglect. That is what we call homeopathic doses. A homeopathic does is the minimum does necessary to treat a problem. In this case, it is about dealing with people in your room by structuring your interaction with them in very small doses. A good example would be if you are traveling to a town where you have somebody that you really want to begin creating benign neglect. They are toxic or they are no longer a good fit in your life and your relationship. Instead of letting them know a month in advance that you are coming into town, email them the night before and let them know, “Hey.  I am going to be in town tomorrow. I am free between 1:00 and 1:30 if you want to get together.” 

Don’t you like that? Then you are limiting the amount of time. Heck, you might get lucky and they might not even read the email until after 1:00 tomorrow. Homeopathic doses is a powerful concept that was given to us by a friend of ours, Ariel Ford, and she suggested this is terms of relationships.

If you want to create the life of your dreams, there are many things – in a couple of the other podcasts, I talk about harmony vs. balance, but one of them is the people you surround yourself with. It makes a huge difference in creating the life of your dreams. People affect your happiness, especially if you let them. The secret to not letting them is to curate the room you desire because the quality of your life depends on the people in your life. That is the theme for Who Is in Your Room. I invite people to pick up a copy of the book. We will make sure there is a link to it here as well. 

This is a game changer for people. I wish I had this book. I wish somebody had written this book when I was 17 because even though I have had, I think, a great life, I would have made different choices throughout my life had I had something like this. And this fits BNI so beautifully because the quality of your chapter depends on the people in your chapter. 

I think we still have about a minute or so, Priscilla. Do you have any thoughts on this topic?

Priscilla:

You know, sometimes, Ivan, you are given the people in your life like I am thinking if you are born into a certain family. You don’t have that much choice, you know. So how do you deal with that?

Ivan:

That is a great question. Here is another tidbit. Let me frame it in your mind. They may be in your life, but their baggage does not have to be. They may be in your life but their baggage does not have to be. That means you are clear in communicating with them. I will give you an example and this is right out of the book. Rick, one of my co-authors, when his mother was alive, he used to call her every week on Sunday and he would have an hour of so conversation with her. He always dreaded those callers because she had a lot of emotional issues, according to Rick, and she was very toxic. She would talk. Really badly about other people. He hated those calls. So one day he said to her, “Mom, I love you very much, and I really want to talk to you every week, but let me tell you, from now on, if you start getting toxic about other people and badmouthing other people, I am going to say to you, ‘Mom, I love you. I will call you next week at the same time. I look forward to talking to you. Bye,’ and I am going to hang up.”

He said he did it for about three weeks and he had to hang up. After that for the rest of their life together, they had wonderful conversations every week, and he felt like their relationship ended on such a positive note when she passed on. He was very clear: You are in my life, but your baggage has to stay out. You are in my room, but the baggage has to stay out. That is the answer to your question, Priscilla. 

Priscilla:

Yeah, that sounds good. Okay, that is helpful.

Ivan:

Well good. Listen, I will talk more about content from the book. If you are interested in hearing me talk about more material from the book, please leave a comment. If you have some other techniques on this strategy that I just explained, feel free to leave a comment. I read all of the comments that get posted up on BNIpodcast.com. Over to you, Priscilla. 

Priscilla:

Okay that is great. Thank you so much. I think that is it for this week. For more of Ivan’s content, you can go to IvanMisner.com. Thank you so much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of the Official BNI Podcast.

      • Shrikant Danke says

        March 18, 2019 at 10:55 am

        Who is in the room ??
        This podcast really helps me on personal level as I can apply the given solution on the people who are part of my life.
        Thanks Ivan.

  2. Yateesh Kukreti says

    March 7, 2019 at 7:32 pm

    Wonderful information. Would work on it.
    Best Regards

  3. Bhuvaneshwari says

    March 7, 2019 at 7:48 pm

    Shared weekly podcast to our BNI group to all get benefitted. Thank you Dr. Ivan.

    Reply

  4. Tiffany Nedrow says

    March 11, 2019 at 12:19 pm

    Great information for personal relationships as well as professional ones. Thanks for sharing.

    Reply

  5. Donna Loeffler says

    March 11, 2019 at 2:09 pm

    Wonderful podcast and book! I am the president of our chapter in Claremont, Ca, the Fiesta Lunch Bunch. It would be great to do a group study of your book, Dr. Misner, and use the worksheets from your website. We did a core values exercise awhile back and defined our chapter’s top 5 core values. We use this information when we invite visitors and also while interviewing prospective members. I believe knowing where we all stand on core values really helps to attract business people who are a great fit for our chapter. Thank you for sharing your wisdom on this topic, it is essential for success in business, and life.

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 12, 2019 at 5:42 pm

      Hi Donna. Thanks for your comments. You can have access to a study guide for a group discussion and a slew of free webinars by going here and signing up. Everything is free:

      https://tinyurl.com/FreeRoomWebinars

      Ivan

      Reply

      • Donna Loeffler says

        March 12, 2019 at 7:34 pm

        Wow! Thank you, this is fantastic!

  6. Carolina says

    March 12, 2019 at 8:00 am

    This is my very favourite podcast so far!!! Am travelling to the US next week and will certainly buy a hard copy of your book to read and share with my teenage children.
    Thanks for the tips, I love your podcasts!

    Carolina from BNI Contacta in Lima, Peru!

    Reply

  7. Mark Lyons says

    March 24, 2019 at 7:43 pm

    Excellent podcast framing and issue and giving solutions. however, as a mental health expert i disagree on one solution. You indicated that the man taught his mother “how to treat him” ( an assertive attitude I preach often to those in my care)

    However, in relationships with others with whom you are not intimately engaged, it seems you want people to avoid and/or make it difficult to interact.

    This may be the answer in the end but I suggest using the same assertive tact as the man did with his mother and see if you can effect the relationship and the behavior the toxic person has toward you

    If it is positive, it may lead to personal growth to their other interpersonal and business relationships.

    Finally, it is also helpful to look at ourselves first and see it we may be contributing to the toxic relationship as well and perhaps we and out toxic member may grow together as we appreciate each other outlook and feelings.

    -Mark Lyons
    BNI Blue Ash Chapter
    Cincinnati, Ohio

    Reply

  8. Sally Onesty says

    March 24, 2019 at 8:39 pm

    “People affect your happiness, especially if you let them. The secret to not letting them is to curate the room you desire because the quality of your life depends on the people in your life. That is the theme for Who Is in Your Room.” This was the most profound statement for me in this podcast and it’s so true.
    Thank you!

    Reply

  9. Lissette Chávez Román says

    March 26, 2019 at 1:27 pm

    Dr. Ivan saludos podría indicarme, cuál es el enlace del libro “Quien esta en tu habitación” Me parece un tema tan importante el cuidar de la gente que nos rodeamos porque sin duda de esa forma nuestra vida estará en equilibrio, gracias por todos sus podcast son fabulosos !! saludos desde México :)

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 26, 2019 at 3:14 pm

      Lissette, here is the link for the book: https://tinyurl.com/WhosInYourRoom

      Ivan

      Reply

  10. Preetha Balakrishnan says

    April 3, 2019 at 8:36 am

    I really liked the statement: ” you are in my life, but your baggage need not be “. Great! It also goes with Dr David Rock’s Neroscience principals of ” how to create new wiring” which I use constantly inu coaching conversations. Thanks Ivan…With regards, Preetha

    Reply

  11. ILEANA GUZMAN TORRES says

    June 7, 2020 at 6:48 pm

    El libro de Quién está en la habitación ?? te abre los ojos sobre a quien le permites estar en tu vida, entorno y de que manera estar, si es toxico alejarlo pero si es positivo, aporta a tu vida, disfrutar cada instante y crecer juntos, ya que nos convertimos en las 5 personas con quien mas convivimos

    Reply

  12. Jim Meadows says

    March 2, 2025 at 9:48 am

    I love the strategy of “homeopathic dose.” Anyone that understands homeopathy realizes that is a brilliant and effective solution to editing out those bad actors in your life.

    Reply
